key_concepts.definition: The radiation level that is accessible during operation, maintenance, or service conditions.
key_concepts.interpretation: AE is compared against AEL (Accessible Emission Limit) to determine laser class.

key_concepts.definition: When laser products emit at multiple wavelengths simultaneously.
key_concepts.rule: See IEC 60825-1:2014/ISH2 for specific additive rules for multi-wavelength analysis.

key_concepts.definition: Sources with angular subtense at the cornea > 1.5 mrad (αmin).
key_concepts.default_method: For wavelengths 400-1400 nm, angle of acceptance may be limited to 100 mrad.
key_concepts.exception: 400-600 nm for durations > 100s: circular-cone angle of acceptance is not limited.
key_concepts.class_3b_note: For Class 3B AELs, angle of acceptance is not limited.

sections.title: Non-uniform, Non-circular or Multiple Apparent Sources

key_principles.rule: Applicable for circularly symmetric images of the apparent source.
key_principles.non_symmetric_case: For non-circularly symmetric profiles, vary angle of acceptance in each dimension.

key_principles.rule: αmax(t) is calculated for the same emission duration t used to determine AEL(t).

key_principles.method: Determine most restrictive (maximum) ratio of AE over AEL by varying angle of acceptance.
key_principles.goal: Find the grouping that maximizes AE/AEL ratio.

example_calculation.scenario: 20-emitter diode array, each contributing 1 mW through 7mm aperture
example_calculation.conclusion: Vertical two-element group has maximum AE/AEL ratio; Class 2 AEL exceeded by factor 1.2

sub_sections.title: Alpha Determination (4.3 f) 3)
sub_sections.alpha_max_rule: αmax(t) limits both C6(α) determination and angle of acceptance γ
sub_sections.time_consistency: Same emission duration t used for AEL(t) and αmax(t)
sub_sections.c5_criterion_exception: For 'Unless α > 100 mrad', α is NOT restricted by αmax
sub_sections.non_uniform_sources: Both angular dimensions must satisfy inequality for C5 = 1

sub_sections.title: T2(α) Calculation
sub_sections.alpha_limit: α limited to max 100 mrad (equivalent to αmax for 0.25s and longer)
sub_sections.arithmetic_mean: Mean value used to determine α; both dimensions need not independently satisfy criterion

sections.title: Groups of Pulses with Duration > Ti

thermal_additivity.principle: When pulses are close together, they thermally group into one 'effective' pulse
thermal_additivity.n_definition: N = number of pulse groups within T2 or time base (whichever is shorter)

analysis_method.ael_single: Determined for pulse group duration t_group
analysis_method.c5_determination: N is number of pulse groups within evaluation duration
analysis_method.ael_application: C5 applied to AELsingle to obtain AELs.p.train

simplification_rule.varying_peak_power: For groups with varying peak power, set t_group equal to integration duration
simplification_rule.fwhm_note: No need to determine group duration based on FWHM criterion

critical_parameters.t2_time_constant: Thermal injury time constant dependent on α
